title: No date range filtering on match queries — only season filter implemented
evidence: tools.go:22-49 search_matches InputSchema defines team1, team2, season, competition, limit but no date_start/date_end parameters; tools.go:224-298 SearchMatches filters by season (line 237) but has no date range logic
suggestion: Add date_start and date_end string parameters to search_matches and filter matches by Date field in SearchMatches

title: TestSearchMatches_TeamAndSeason conditionally skips when 2023 data absent
evidence: mcp_test.go:210 t.Skip("No Palmeiras matches in 2023 - dataset may not include this year")
suggestion: Use a season known to exist in the dataset (e.g. 2019) to avoid conditional skip

title: TestGetTeamStats_HomeRecord conditionally skips when 2022 data absent
suggestion: Use a season known to exist in the dataset to avoid conditional skip

title: BR-Football-Dataset matches loaded without season field
evidence: data.go:208-234 LoadBRFootballDataset does not extract a season from the CSV; Match.Season defaults to 0 for these ~10k records, making them invisible to season-based queries
suggestion: Parse the year from the date field and set Match.Season for BR-Football-Dataset records

title: Zero external dependencies — pure stdlib Go implementation
evidence: go.mod:1-3 has no require directives; only uses encoding/csv, encoding/json, bufio, io, os, sort, strings, strconv, time, log, fmt, regexp, path/filepath
suggestion: Positive quality signal for maintainability and portability
